title: Curiosity became engineering practice
summary: The journey started with code, electronics, mechanical intuition, and the habit of turning abstract ideas into small working tests.
evidence: Started with Python, Arduino sketches, serial commands, and hardware debugging.
Began tinkering with electronics and different microcontrollers.
Learned to treat mistakes as data instead of dead ends.

title: Started Working on ROS 2 for the eYantra Robotics Competition
summary: My first experience with Ubuntu and the wild world of ROS 2, Gazebo, and RViz.
evidence: Learned dual-booting, Linux fundamentals, SSH, and how to work with a team for the first time.
Started working on simulations in Gazebo, explored TurtleBot tutorials, and finally cleared Round 1 of the eYantra RC.
Eventually implemented our own ROS 2 rover based on the Waveshare UGV Rover.

title: Craziness of Airlines
summary: Joined a US-based startup as an intern, working on reducing fuel costs for airlines.
evidence: Started by making simple dashboards in Power BI and Amazon QuickSight.
Analyzed more than 5 million flight records, including flight plans, loadsheets, OAG schedules, and ADS-B tracking data.
Developed natural-language intelligent chatbots that could query airlines' proprietary flight and ground-operations data within seconds.
Explored the world of weight and balance for narrowbody, widebody, and regional aircraft.
Made production-ready simulations for individual flights.
Had the honor of connecting with industry leaders and working with amazing people.

title: My First IITD, Robocon, and Travel Experience
summary: Joined Team IUST MechBots on 2 July 2026, one month before the Robocon Finals.
evidence: Had the privilege of representing the first-ever team from Kashmir at the Robocon Finals.
The rover assembly was finished one week before the finals, which gave me one week for programming and testing.
An accumulation of software, mechanical, and mainly electrical flaws led to a zero score in both matches.
The best experience of my life so far; everything we did during those forty days felt like a pilgrimage, and I felt alive again.
